originalTitle: Erling Haaland hails ‘one of the sickest days’ in Norway’s history after beating Brazil
summary: Erling Haaland said he wished he was part of the street celebrations in Norway after what his coach, Ståle Solbakken, called ‘the greatest day’ in his country’s football history
content: Ståle Solbakken called it “the greatest day in Norwegian football history” while Erling Haaland labelled it one of “the sickest”. One thing is for certain, however: Norway are through to the quarter-finals of the World Cup for the first time after deservedly beating Brazil. A late double from Haaland decided the contest, with the final score 2-1, and immediately caused chaos back home. Tens of thousands took to the streets of Oslo in the early hours to celebrate, including an impromptu gathering at the royal palace, where fans were greeted by Crown Prince ‌Haakon, who was wearing a Norway scarf. “I think that all Norwegian citizens are experiencing the night of a lifetime,” Solbakken, the coach, said. “They will probably party for a week or so. Erling was our match-winner but I’m happy that everyone played their game on the greatest night in Norwegian football history. Everyone played their part. “This is a great group. They love being together, they train well, they help each other and protect each other. We have a strong culture and we have an opportunity to let people be themselves and say whatever they want. That’s very important both when things go well and when they don’t.” Almost as if hearing his coach’s words, Haaland was being himself in his post-match remarks. “I ‌wish I was on those streets; I wish I was involved in it,” Haaland said of the celebrations. “Everyone has to enjoy themselves. All of Norway has to enjoy themselves. It’s one of the sickest days ever in Norwegian history. You just have to enjoy it, because this is sick.” A half-time double substitution was key to changing the tide of a closely fought contest, with Solbakken saying switching his wingers had been an instinctive decision to increase possession in dangerous areas. “You have to have faith in what you do,” he said. “You have to follow your gut feeling. It could go both ways. It was a chess game.” Neymar had scored for Brazil with a penalty deep in added time, while Bruno Guimarães had a first-half spot-kick saved with the score at 0-0. Carlo Ancelotti, who became Brazil manager just over a year ago, must deal with the heat of a post-tournament inquisition. As their former talisman Neymar appeared to announce his international retirement post-match – “I tried, I tried … Now it’s over,” he said – Ancelotti insisted the team must draft a new generation of players into the Seleção. “Everyone is profoundly disappointed,” Ancelotti said. “I think we didn’t do a spectacular World Cup but we did a good one and we even deserved to win this match at some points. This is a defeat, and we have to continue doing our jobs continuing to improve. It’s not the end, it’s the beginning of a new cycle.”
chineseTitle: 击败巴西队后，埃尔林·哈兰德称赞这是挪威历史上“最糟糕的日子之一”
chineseSummary: 埃尔林·哈兰德表示，在他的教练斯托尔·索尔巴肯称挪威足球历史上“最伟大的一天”之后，他希望自己能成为挪威街头庆祝活动的一部分
chineseContent: 斯塔尔·索尔巴肯称其为“挪威足球史上最伟大的一天”，而埃尔林·哈兰德则将其称为“最病态的一天”。然而，有一点是肯定的：挪威队当之无愧地击败了巴西队，首次进入世界杯四分之一决赛。哈兰德最后时刻的梅开二度决定了比赛的胜负，最终比分2-1，立即在主场引起了混乱。清晨，数万人走上奥斯陆街头庆祝，其中包括在皇宫举行的即兴聚会，粉丝们受到了戴着挪威围巾的哈康王储的欢迎。 “我认为所有挪威公民都在经历一生难忘的夜晚，”教练索尔巴肯说。 “他们可能会聚会一周左右。埃尔林是我们比赛的获胜者，但我很高兴每个人都在挪威足球历史上最伟大的夜晚踢出了自己的比赛。每个人都发挥了自己的作用。“这是一个伟大的团队。他们喜欢在一起，训练有素，互相帮助，互相保护。我们拥有强大的文化，我们有机会让人们做自己并畅所欲言。无论事情进展顺利还是进展不顺利，这一点都非常重要。”哈兰德在赛后的讲话中几乎就像听到了教练的话一样。 “我希望我在那些街道上；我希望我能参与其中，”哈兰德谈到庆祝活动时说道。“每个人都必须享受其中。整个挪威都应该尽情享受。这是挪威历史上最严重的日子之一。你只需要享受它，因为这很病态。”半场时的双换人是改变这场势均力敌的比赛潮流的关键，索尔巴肯表示，换边锋是本能的决定，目的是增加危险区域的控球率。 “你必须有fai
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
